[151:39]I said it's not an ambush because there's no surprise.

[151:51]I'm not sure they reassured me. They said, no, it's going to be live. It's going to

[151:56]be a live interview, two hours, one on one, no gimmicks. And I said, great, I think that's

[152:02]great. I was glad that it was live because then I can be confident that it won't be edited

[152:08]or censored or they're not going to mess with it in production. I said, that's fine.

[152:22]He played all my clips, all the greatest hits and basically asked me if I agreed with myself.

[152:33]It was another clip show.

[152:38]You know how this goes.

[152:52]it is and they'll take something that's supposed to be a joke or it's rhetorical or whatever.

[152:59]And then they treat you like you're in a police interrogation. They treat you like you're

[153:04]and they say, you submitted that on the record.

[153:06]And they act like you swore under oath

[153:08]that everything you've ever said is serious.

[153:15]They play the greatest hits,

[153:16]the things that sound the worst

[153:18]and they ask you to defend it.

[153:23]They said, he's playing you clips from your own show

[153:26]and then asking you if you agree with yourself.

[153:29]It's like, of course I'm going to agree with myself.

[182:38]hating ourselves, hating our ancestors,

[182:41]having to apologize, rejecting the self,

[182:45]elevating and supporting the other,

[182:56]the oppressed, the marginalized, the dysfunctional, whatever.

[184:30]idea, what if we took the chains off?

[184:47]to think freely, act freely, to do what is best for ourselves,

[184:53]to take care of ourselves, to be proud of ourselves,

[184:56]to be proud of our children, to be proud of our ancestors,

[184:58]to be proud of who we are.
